reasoning: The NFL is highly competitive, and no team has an overwhelming chance to win a Super Bowl two seasons ahead. Although the Chiefs are a dominant franchise right now with Patrick Mahomes at quarterback, looking ahead to the 2025-2026 season brings several variables. The best teams typically have around a 10-15% implied probability of winning the Super Bowl before the season starts. For a specific Super Bowl that is two seasons away, factoring in the risk of injuries (especially to key players like Mahomes), potential roster turnover, and the general unpredictability of the league, the odds generally converge to around 10-12%. Taking everything into consideration, I estimate the Chiefs' chances of winning Super Bowl LX at approximately 12%.
